title: Bulk Import from JSON

story.i_want_to: import a complete project work breakdown (epics, stories, and dependencies) from a JSON file in a single API call
story.so_that: I can bootstrap a project's tracking state from an external source (user story JSON files, PRD tooling output, or another loopctl instance) without manually creating each entity

rationale: Real projects have 25+ epics and 185+ stories. Manually creating each via individual API calls is impractical. Bulk import allows the entire work breakdown structure to be loaded atomically — either everything succeeds or nothing does. This is the primary onboarding path for new projects and the foundation for round-trip import/export (US-12.3).

acceptance_criteria.description: POST /api/v1/projects/:id/import accepts a JSON body containing: epics (array of epic objects) and story_dependencies (array of dependency edges). Each epic contains: number, title, description, phase, position, metadata, and stories (array of story objects). Each story contains: number, title, description, acceptance_criteria, estimated_hours, metadata.

The entire import is executed in a single database transaction (Ecto.Multi). If any part fails (validation error, constraint violation, cycle detection), the entire import is rolled back and nothing is persisted.
Story dependencies are specified as pairs of story numbers (e.g., {story: '2.1', depends_on: '1.3'}). Numbers are resolved to IDs after all stories are created within the transaction.
The import validates the dependency graph for cycles before committing. If a cycle is detected, the entire import is rolled back with a 422 error describing the cycle.
All imported entities are created with tenant_id matching the authenticated tenant. All stories start with agent_status=pending and verified_status=unverified.
On success, the response includes a summary: {epics_created: N, stories_created: N, dependencies_created: N}.
The import emits a project.imported webhook event (if webhooks are configured) in the same transaction.
Validation errors include the specific location of the failure (e.g., 'epics[2].stories[3].title: can't be blank') for easy debugging.
Duplicate epic numbers or story numbers within the import payload are rejected with a 422 error.
The endpoint requires the user role. The project must belong to the authenticated tenant (enforced by RLS).

test_cases.name: successful import with epics, stories, and dependencies
steps: POST /api/v1/projects/:id/import with JSON body containing 2 epics, 4 stories (2 per epic), and 1 story dependency
expected_results: Response is 201 Created
Response body includes epics_created=2, stories_created=4, dependencies_created=1
All epics exist in database with correct project_id and tenant_id
All stories exist with agent_status=pending, verified_status=unverified
The story dependency is correctly created linking the two stories
Audit log entries exist for all created entities

test_cases.name: import rolls back on validation error
steps: POST /api/v1/projects/:id/import with JSON body where the second epic has a story with blank title
expected_results: Response is 422 Unprocessable Entity
Error message includes 'epics[1].stories[N].title: can't be blank' (or similar path)
No epics or stories were created (entire transaction rolled back)
No audit log entries were created

test_cases.name: cycle detection rejects circular dependencies
steps: POST /api/v1/projects/:id/import with dependencies forming a cycle: A depends on B, B depends on C, C depends on A
expected_results: Response is 422 Unprocessable Entity
Error message indicates a dependency cycle was detected
Error includes the cycle path (e.g., 'Cycle detected: 1.1 -> 1.2 -> 1.3 -> 1.1')
Nothing is persisted

test_cases.name: duplicate story numbers rejected
steps: POST /api/v1/projects/:id/import with two stories having the same number '1.1'
expected_results: Response is 422 Unprocessable Entity
Error message indicates duplicate story number

test_cases.name: tenant isolation on import
steps: POST /api/v1/projects/<tenant_b_project_id>/import with Tenant A's API key
expected_results: Response is 404 Not Found (project not visible to Tenant A via RLS)

test_cases.name: webhook event emitted on successful import
expected_results: Import succeeds with 201
webhook_event exists with event_type='project.imported'
Event payload includes project_id, epic_count, story_count

test_cases.name: dependency references non-existent story number
steps: POST /api/v1/projects/:id/import with a dependency referencing story number '99.99' that doesn't exist in the payload
expected_results: Response is 422 Unprocessable Entity
Error message indicates unresolved dependency reference
Nothing is persisted

test_cases.name: agent role cannot import
expected_results: Response is 403 Forbidden

technical_notes: Use Ecto.Multi to create all epics, stories, and dependencies in a single transaction. Steps: validate structure -> create epics -> create stories (referencing epic IDs from earlier multi steps) -> resolve dependency story numbers to IDs -> detect cycles -> create dependencies -> create audit log entries -> generate webhook events.
Cycle detection: build an adjacency list from the dependency edges and run a topological sort (Kahn's algorithm or DFS with cycle detection). If the sort cannot complete, there is a cycle.
Story number resolution: build a map of story_number -> story_id from the multi's intermediate results, then resolve dependency references against this map.
Validation path tracking: use accumulators to track the JSON path (e.g., epics[0].stories[2]) through the import structure so validation errors include location context.
Import context module: Loopctl.Import with import_project/3 function that accepts (project, json_data, actor). Returns {:ok, summary} or {:error, changeset_or_message}.
Controller: ImportController or add import action to ProjectController. Route: POST /api/v1/projects/:id/import.
The import JSON format should match the export format (US-12.2) for round-trip fidelity. Design the schema now with export in mind.
Consider adding epic_dependencies support in the import payload alongside story_dependencies.
Payload size limit: consider a max import size (e.g., 10MB) to prevent abuse.
